name: protein_binder_boltz2
description: De novo binder design against the IgV domain of human PD-L1 (CD274, UniProt Q9NZQ7 residues 19-127, as resolved in PDB 5IUS chain B; Lin et al. 2008, Zak et al. 2017). Two constructs: `binder_construct` holds the variable 80 aa binder, and `target_construct` holds the fixed PD-L1 IgV target. The `boltz2-binding-strength` constraint takes both segments as inputs - Boltz2 (Wohlwend et al. 2025) co-folds the heterodimer and returns confidence metrics (iptm, iplddt, ipde, complex_plddt, complex_ptm); the default protein-protein weight scheme rewards high iptm and low ipde, i.e. confident interface predictions. Stage 1 uses rejection-sampling with ESM2-650M at temperature 1.0 and a 3-mutation budget for broad exploration, scoring with binding-strength + binder-only pLDDT + protein-complexity (so the optimizer doesn't drift toward low-complexity glycine/serine soup). Stage 2 narrows to single-mutation MCMC with ESM2 at temperature 0.7 and retains the same constraint set. The 80 aa fixed length sits in the 60-100 aa range of de-novo binders from Cao et al. 2022 (RFdiffusion); ESM2 substitutes residues without changing length so a `protein-length` constraint would be a no-op. The starting binder is a synthetic 80 aa all-alpha mini-protein scaffold (acidic-residue-biased de-novo helical bundle, used here only to give MCMC a defined-length seed); replace with your own designed seed (e.g. an RFdiffusion or BindCraft scaffold) for a real binder campaign. Companion gradient-based example for the same target: `germinal_pdl1_binder.json`.

segments.label: 80 aa synthetic all-alpha mini-protein binder seed
Human PD-L1 IgV domain (UniProt Q9NZQ7 res 19-127, PDB 5IUS chain B)
